name: Easy Chinese Beef Stir Fry
content:
# Easy Chinese Beef Stir Fry

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/easy-classic-chinese-beef-stir-fry/) | **Author:** Nagi Maehashi | **Rating:** 4.93 from 148 votes | **Servings:** 3–4

---

## Overview

A classic Chinese beef stir fry with a glossy, restaurant-quality sauce and **velvety tender beef** using the Chinese restaurant velveting technique. Originally published December 2015, republished October 2025 with refined process and the key addition of always velveting the beef.

> "This Beef Stir Fry is made with a simple but super flavourful, classic Chinese stir fry sauce that tastes just like you get at proper Chinese restaurants."

**Prep:** 15 min | **Cook:** 8 min | **Total:** 23 min

---

## Key Technique: Velveting the Beef

The **secret to restaurant-quality tender beef** in stir fries. Without it, beef overcooks and becomes chewy during the ~6 minute stir fry process.

**Method:**
1. Slice beef thinly (3–4mm / 1/6" thick, 4–5 cm / 2" long)
2. Toss with **1/2 teaspoon baking soda**, set aside for **20 minutes (no longer)**
3. Rinse under cold tap water in a colander
4. Shake off excess water, blot with paper towels (doesn't need to be fully dry)

> "A tiny extra step that makes a huge difference – try it once and you'll never look back!"

**⚠️ Warning:** Don't exceed 20 minutes or beef becomes over-tenderised and unpleasantly soft.

---

## Ingredients

### Velveting
| Ingredient | Amount |
|---|---|
| Beef rump steak (or flank) | 7 oz / 200 g |
| Baking soda | 1/2 tsp |

### Sauce
| Ingredient | Amount | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Light soy sauce | 2 tbsp | NOT dark soy |
| Oyster sauce | 1 tbsp | Or vegetarian alternative |
| Chinese cooking wine (Shaoxing) | 2 tbsp | Sub: mirin, sake, dry sherry |
| White sugar | 1 tsp | |
| Sesame oil (toasted/brown) | 1/2 tsp | |
| White pepper | Pinch | Or black pepper |

### Sauce Thickener
| Ingredient | Amount |
|---|---|
| Cornstarch | 3 tsp |
| Water | 1/2 cup |

### Stir Fry
| Ingredient | Amount |
|---|---|
| Peanut oil | 1 1/2 tbsp |
| Garlic, minced | 1 clove |
| Onion, sliced | 1/2 small |
| Red capsicum, sliced | 1/2 |
| Carrot, diagonal slices | 1 small |
| Bok choy (stems & leaves separated) | 2 |
| Green onion, 1.5" pieces | 2 |

---

## Instructions

1. **Mix sauce** (except cornflour). Take **1.5 tbsp** and toss with velveted beef.
2. **Mix cornflour** into remaining sauce until lump-free, then add water.
3. **Heat oil** on high heat in wok/large non-stick pan. Cook **onion + garlic** for 10 seconds.
4. **Add beef**, stir constantly for **1 1/2 min** until red → brown.
5. **Add firm vegetables:** carrot, capsicum, bok choy stems, white green onion. Stir **1 1/2 min**.
6. **Pour sauce**, stir until bubbling.
7. **Add leafy vegetables:** bok choy leaves + green onion tops. Toss **1 min** until sauce thickens, glossy, leaves just wilted.
8. **Serve immediately** over steamed rice.

---

## Ingredient Substitutions & Notes

### Beef Cuts
- **Best:** Rump (or rump cap/picanha/coulotte roast – better value), flank, scotch fillet, porterhouse/sirloin
- **Slow cooking cuts** (chuck, oyster blade): Use heaped 1/2 tsp baking soda, tenderise 35 min
- **Other proteins:** Chicken thigh (no tenderising), chicken breast (20 min), pork/lamb (20–30 min)

### Sauce Shortcut
Use **5 1/2 tablespoons of "Charlie"** (All-Purpose Stir Fry Sauce) instead of making from scratch.

### Gluten-Free / Alcohol-Free
- For alcohol-free: substitute Chinese cooking wine + half the water with low-sodium chicken stock

---

## Pro Tips

- **Velveting transforms tough cuts** – one reader noted: *"a tough old rump transformed into delicious tenderness"*
- **Rump roast (rump cap/picanha)** is better value than steak and allows larger, restaurant-style slices
- **Separate bok choy stems and leaves** – stems go in earlier as they take longer to cook
- **Separate green onion whites and greens** – whites cook earlier
- **Leftovers:** Keep 3–4 days; sauce thins when reheated. Use **arrowroot** instead of cornflour if making ahead
- **Vegetable flexibility:** Use what you have – broccoli/cauliflower need pre-cooking first

---

## Nutrition (per serving, 4 servings)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|---|---|
| Calories | 159 cal |
| Protein | 13 g |
| Carbohydrates | 7 g |
| Fat | 8 g |
| Sodium | 683 mg |
| Fiber | 1 g |

name: Beef in Black Bean Sauce
content:
# Beef in Black Bean Sauce

**Author:** Nagi Maehashi
**Published:** 9 Apr '25 | **Updated:** 20 Apr '26
**Servings:** 4–5 people
**Total Time:** 1 hour 30 minutes (20 min prep + 10 min cook + 1 hr marinating)
**Rating:** 4.92 from 75 votes

> *"This incredible Beef in Black Bean Sauce recipe is from Hannah, a chef at RecipeTin Meals... and it's the best I've ever had!"* – Nagi

---

## Key Highlights

- **Authentic & Restaurant-Quality**: Developed by Chef Hannah (born/raised in China, multigenerational chef family), this recipe rivals or beats high-end Chinese restaurants.
- **Cost-Effective**: Homemade version costs **~$15–25** vs. **$30+** for a smaller restaurant portion — **up to 70% cheaper**.
- **From Scratch Superiority**: Sauce made from scratch with real preserved black beans — far better than jarred versions.
- **Economical Beef Option**: Blade roast or chuck (65% cheaper than rump) works **95% as good** with adjusted marination.

---

## Core Ingredients

### 1. Preserved Black Beans *(Essential)*
- **What they are**: Fermented black soybeans (not Western black beans!).
- **Also known as**: Salted/fermented black beans, douchi (豆豉).
- **Where to buy**: Asian grocery stores (~$2.70/pack), near pickled/vac-packed veggies.
- **Texture**: Wrinkly, squishy like firm raisins.
- **No substitute** — critical for authentic flavor.
- **Soak**: 30–60 mins before use to soften and release flavor.

### 2. Beef & Marinade

#### Best Cuts:
- **Premium**: Rump steak (US: top sirloin), scotch/rib eye, porterhouse/sirloin.
- **Budget-Friendly (65% cheaper)**: Blade roast or chuck — **excellent results** with longer marination.

#### Marinade (per 400g/14oz beef):
- 1 tbsp light soy sauce
- 1 tsp dark soy sauce
- 1 tbsp oyster sauce *(or vegetarian "oyster" sauce)*
- 2 tsp cornflour/cornstarch
- ¼ tsp baking soda *(key tenderizer!)*
- 1 tbsp sesame oil *(added last to "seal" marinade)*

> **Pro Tip**: For budget cuts, marinate **24 hours** (vs. 1 hour) OR use baking soda tenderizing method: toss beef with 1¼ tsp baking soda, refrigerate 40 mins, rinse well, then marinate 30 mins (without baking soda).

### 3. Stir-Fry & Sauce
- **Vegetables**: Onion & green capsicum (bell pepper), cut into 2.5cm/1" squares; garlic (minced).
- **Aromatics**: Black beans, garlic, onion, capsicum.
- **Sauce**:
  - 1 tbsp light soy sauce
  - 2 tsp sugar
  - 2 tbsp cornflour
  - 1 cup water
- **Cooking Wine**: Chinese cooking wine (Shaoxing wine) — essential for restaurant-quality flavor.
  *Substitutes*: Dry sherry, cooking sake, or non-alcoholic: replace half water with low-sodium chicken broth.
- **Oil**: Peanut, canola, or vegetable oil (neutral flavor).

---

## Step-by-Step Instructions

### 1. Marinate Beef *(1 hour)*
- Slice beef **3mm / 0.1" thick**.
- *Restaurant-style tip*: Cut steak into 2.5 x 4cm blocks, stand upright, then slice for larger pieces.
- Mix all marinade ingredients **except sesame oil**. Add beef, coat well. Add sesame oil last, toss.
- Refrigerate **1 hour** (24 hours for budget cuts).

### 2. Soak Black Beans *(30–60 mins)*
- Cover beans in water, soak, then drain.

### 3. Prepare Sauce
- Whisk cornflour, soy sauce, sugar, and water until smooth. Set aside.

### 4. Cook *(<10 mins)*
1. **Shallow-fry beef**: Heat ½ cup oil in wok over high heat. Cook beef **30–60 sec** until browned. Remove with slotted spoon.
   *(Healthier option: Sauté in 2 tbsp oil for 45 sec)*
2. **Discard oil**, keep **3 tbsp**.
3. **Cook aromatics**:
   - Black beans: 20 sec
   - Garlic: 10 sec
   - Onion + capsicum: 1 min (until edges soften)
4. **Add beef + wine**: Toss beef 1 min, pour Shaoxing wine around wok rim (traditional technique to "cook" alcohol).
5. **Add sauce**: Stir 1 min until thick, shiny, and coats beef.
6. **Serve immediately** with steamed white rice.

---

## Pro Tips & Notes

- **Baking soda** is a "magic" meat tenderizer — prevents toughness.
- **Dark soy sauce** adds color and depth; don't substitute with light soy.
- **Oyster sauce** adds umami — use shellfish-free version if needed.
- **Wok preferred** for oil depth, but large deep pan works.
- **Leftovers**: Keep 3 days in fridge (reheats well). **Not suitable for freezing** (sauce thins).

---

## Nutrition per serving (5 servings, no rice)

**421 cal** | 24g protein | 22g fat | 16g carbs | 1740mg sodium (76% DV)

name: Thai Sweet Chilli Beef Bowls
content:
# Thai Sweet Chilli Beef Bowls

**Author:** Nagi Maehashi
**Published:** 4 Dec '24 | **Updated:** 18 Jun '25
**Rating:** 4.92 from 58 votes
**Servings:** 4
**Total Time:** 20 minutes (13 min prep + 7 min cook)

---

## Overview

A quick, flavorful dinner inspired by Thai chilli basil stir fry and poke bowl concepts—**without basil or raw fish**. Uses beef mince and bottled sweet chilli sauce for speed and kid-friendly mildness. The star is the **Sweet Chilli Drizzle Sauce**, which ties together rice, beef, and any raw or cooked vegetables.

> "It's really tasty. I hope you love it!" – Nagi

---

## Key Features

- **Fast & versatile**: Ready in 20 minutes; works with any veggies on hand
- **Not authentic Thai**, but uses staple Thai ingredients (fish sauce, oyster sauce, garlic, sugar)
- **Two distinct sauces**: One cooked (caramelized), one fresh (drizzled)
- **Kid-friendly**: Mild heat, customizable toppings
- **Meal-prep friendly**: Beef keeps 3–4 days fridge / 3 months freezer

---

## Ingredients

### Sweet Chilli Stir Fry Sauce (cooked)
- 2 tbsp sweet chilli sauce (e.g., Trident)
- 2 tbsp fish sauce
- 2 tbsp rice vinegar (or cider vinegar)
- 1 tbsp oyster sauce
- 1 tbsp dark soy sauce (or light/all-purpose soy)

### Sweet Chilli Drizzle Sauce (raw/fresh)
- 1 garlic clove, minced
- 3 tbsp sweet chilli sauce
- 2 tbsp lime juice (or rice vinegar)
- 2 tsp fish sauce

### Beef Stir Fry
- 500g / 1 lb beef mince (any fat %)
- 1 small onion, finely chopped
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp canola oil

### Toppings & Sides
- ⅓ cup roasted cashews (or peanuts/almonds), toasted & chopped
- 3 tbsp chopped coriander/cilantro (or green onion)
- Jasmine rice (or plain rice, quinoa, cauliflower rice)
- Raw or cooked veggies: cucumber, red onion, broccoli, carrot, radish, edamame, etc.

---

## Instructions

1. **Mix both sauces** in separate bowls.
2. **Toast cashews** in dry pan (~2 min), then chop.
3. **Sauté** onion + garlic 1 min over high heat.
4. **Cook beef**, breaking up until no red remains.
5. **Add stir fry sauce**, cook 3–4 min until reduced and caramelized.
6. **Assemble bowls**: rice + beef + veggies + drizzle sauce + cashews + coriander.
7. **Mix vigorously** before eating!

> "Don't skimp on this step as the caramelisation of the sauce on the beef is where the flavour is!"

---

## Pro Tips & Substitutions

- **Fish sauce**: Smells strong but cooks out—don't skip! Sub with light soy if needed.
- **Dark soy sauce**: Adds rich color and depth; light soy works in a pinch.
- **Lime juice**: Use rice vinegar if limes are expensive.
- **Nuts**: Toasting is essential for flavor. Salted nuts? Reduce fish sauce by ½ tbsp.
- **Veggies**: Any raw or steamed veg works—the drizzle sauce makes them all tasty!
- **Proteins**: Chicken, turkey, or pork mince also work well.

---

## Nutrition (per serving)

- **Calories**: 585 kcal (206 from rice)
- **Protein**: 33g
- **Carbs**: 62g
- **Fat**: 22g
- **Sodium**: 1617mg

> *Use cauliflower rice or quinoa to reduce calories/carbs.*

name: Qeema (Indian Curried Beef Mince)
content:
# Qeema – Easy Indian Curried Beef Mince

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/qeema-indian-curried-beef/) | **Author:** Nagi Maehashi | **Rating:** 4.87/5 (101 votes) | **226 Comments**

---

## Overview

> *"This is an authentic Indian curried beef mince recipe called Qeema (or keema or kheema). It's a gem of a find because it tastes incredible but unlike many Indian dishes, there are no hard-to-find spices in the ingredients. And it's super fast – on the table in 20 minutes!"*

- **Prep:** 5 minutes | **Cook:** 15 minutes | **Total:** 20 minutes
- **Servings:** 4
- **Cuisine:** Indian | **Course:** Dinner
- Also known as: keema, kheema — a home cooking meal (not typically found on restaurant menus)

---

## Ingredients

### Main
- 3 tbsp vegetable or canola oil (or other neutral oil)
- 4 tsp ginger, finely minced
- 5 large garlic cloves, minced (~4 tsp)
- 1 large onion, finely diced
- 500g / 1 lb beef mince (ground beef)
- ¾ tsp cooking/kosher salt
- 1 tsp cayenne pepper or chilli powder (pure, **not** US chili powder spice mix) — omit for not spicy
- 1¼ tsp garam masala
- 1¼ tsp cumin powder
- 1¼ tsp coriander powder
- ½ tsp turmeric powder
- 1 cup water

### Garnish
- 1 green cayenne pepper, deseeded, finely sliced
- Cilantro/coriander leaves
- Plain yogurt or Mint Yogurt (recipe below)

### Mint Yogurt (optional but highly recommended)
- ¾ cup plain yogurt
- ½ cup (lightly packed) mint leaves
- ¼ tsp cooking/kosher salt

---

## Instructions

1. **Sauté** — Heat oil in a skillet over high heat. Add ginger and garlic, sauté 30 seconds until golden (don't let it burn!). Add onion and cook 1 minute until starting to turn translucent.

2. **Add beef** — Cook, breaking it up, until it changes from pink to light brown. Add remaining ingredients **EXCEPT water**. Cook 2 more minutes to let the spices bloom.

3. **Cook 10 minutes** — Add water, stir, then cover with a lid (or baking tray). Turn heat to medium and simmer 10 minutes until most water has evaporated but still a bit juicy.

4. **Serve** — Over basmati rice or plain white rice, garnished with extra chilli, coriander/cilantro, and lots of Mint Yogurt.

### Mint Yogurt Method
> *"Blitz then stir — Put just ¼ cup of yogurt with the mint leaves and salt in a jug just big enough to fit the head of a stick blender. Blitz until mint is very finely chopped. Then stir in remaining yogurt."*

---

## Key Recipe Notes

1. **American "Chili Powder"** is not pure ground chilli — it contains other spices like paprika and is not very spicy. This recipe calls for pure ground chilli or cayenne pepper.
2. **Garam Masala** — Described as the *"better curry powder"* because it tastes more legit; sold at regular grocery stores.
3. **Roti** — Flaky Indian round flatbread sold in freezer sections; cook from frozen on the stove in minutes. *"Cheap, tasty, if you've never tried it, it's a game changer!"*
4. **Mint yogurt** — Blitzing makes yogurt watery, so only blitz the minimum to puree the mint, then stir in the rest to re-thicken.
5. **Leftovers** — Keep 3–4 days in fridge, or freeze for 3 months.

---

## Serving Suggestions

- **Rice:** Basmati or plain white rice
- **Breads:** Naan, flatbreads, or frozen roti
- **Mint yogurt** (strongly recommended — multiple reviewers said it "makes the dish")

---

## Nutrition (per serving, beef only)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|---|---|
| Calories | 255 cal |
| Protein | 28 g |
| Carbohydrates | 4 g |
| Fat | 14 g |
| Saturated Fat | 3 g |
| Cholesterol | 78 mg |
| Sodium | 525 mg |
| Potassium | 517 mg |
| Iron | 4 mg |

name: Beef Chow Mein
content:
# Beef Chow Mein

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/beef-chow-mein-noodles/) | **Author:** Nagi Maehashi | **Rating:** 4.90 from 76 votes | **Serves:** 2 generously

---

## Overview

A quick, economical beef mince (ground beef) stir-fry noodle dish — the beef version of the popular Chicken Chow Mein. Features hidden vegetables, a caramelised beef technique, and a clever egg-scrambling trick to make beef cling to noodles.

> *"The beef is stir fried with chow mein sauce until it's beautifully caramelised then tossed in a tangle of noodles and vegetables."*

---

## Ingredients

### Noodles & Add-ins
- **200g / 7 oz chow mein noodles** (or any thin yellow egg noodles, ramen cakes, or even spaghetti)
- **200g / 7 oz beef mince** (any fat %; lean won't caramelise as well)
- **2 garlic cloves**, finely minced
- **1 egg** — scrambled into beef to help it stick to noodles + protein boost
- **3 green onion stems**, cut into 5cm / 2" lengths (white & green parts separated)
- **2 cups green cabbage**, finely sliced (or Chinese cabbage)
- **1 carrot**, cut into thin batons
- **1 heaped cup bean sprouts** (~75g / 2½ oz)
- **1½ tbsp canola oil**

### Chow Mein Sauce
- **1½ tbsp light soy sauce** (or all-purpose soy sauce)
- **1½ tsp dark soy sauce** (stains noodles mahogany colour + adds soy flavour)
- **1½ tbsp oyster sauce**
- **1½ tbsp Chinese cooking wine** (Shaoxing wine)
- **1½ tsp white sugar**
- **Pinch white pepper**

### Sauce Thickener
- **1½ tsp cornflour / cornstarch**
- **3 tbsp water**

---

## Instructions

1. **Mix sauce** — Combine all sauce ingredients in a bowl; set aside
2. **Make slurry** — Mix cornflour and water in a separate bowl
3. **Cook noodles** — Per packet directions, then drain
4. **Caramelise beef** — Heat oil in large non-stick pan on high heat. Cook garlic 10 sec, then add beef, breaking it up. Cook until no pink remains. Add **white parts of green onions + 1½ tbsp of the Sauce**. Cook **2 more minutes** for caramelisation
5. **Finish sauce** — Mix cornflour slurry into remaining sauce
6. **Scramble egg** — Add egg to pan, mix through beef (scrambles and helps beef stick to noodles)
7. **Add veg** — When egg mostly cooked, add carrot and cabbage. Cook **1 minute** until cabbage wilts
8. **Combine** — Add bean sprouts, noodles, and sauce. Toss **1 minute**. Add green parts of green onions. Toss **1 minute more**. Serve.

---

## Key Tips & Insights

### The Egg Trick
> *"Scrambling up an egg with the beef is a neat trick to make the beef cling to the noodles better."*

### Hidden Vegetables
> *"A carrot, 2 heaped cups of cabbage and a heaped cup of bean sprouts. That's a good veg serving for a meal!"*

### Bean Sprout Storage
> *"Keep bean sprouts in water in an airtight container. Change the water every couple of days. This will increase the shelf life of beansprouts 3x."*

### Noodle Flexibility
> *"This dish can really be made with any noodles – or even spaghetti (yes really, who's going to know once tossed in chow mein sauce??)"*

---

## Substitutions & Notes

| Ingredient | Substitute |
|---|---|
| Dark soy sauce | Use only light/all-purpose soy sauce (but **never** only dark — too strong) |
| Oyster sauce | Vegetarian oyster sauce or **hoisin** (slight five-spice note) |
| Chinese cooking wine (Shaoxing) | Mirin, cooking sake, or dry sherry |
| Non-alcoholic option | Replace both cooking wine AND water with low-sodium chicken broth |
| Beef mince | Chicken, pork, turkey, or lamb mince |

---

## Nutrition (per serving — serves 2)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|---|---|
| Calories | 664 cal |
| Carbohydrates | 71 g |
| Protein | 32 g |
| Fat | 30 g |
| Sodium | 1681 mg |
| Fiber | 6 g |

name: Doner Kebab Meat (Beef or Lamb)
content:
# Doner Kebab Meat (Beef or Lamb)

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/doner-kebab-meat-recipe-beef-or-lamb/) | **Author:** Nagi Maehashi

---

## Overview

A homemade version of the classic Turkish doner kebab, made with seasoned beef or lamb mince formed into a loaf and baked. Sliced thinly after cooking, just like the rotating spit version served at kebab shops.

---

## Ingredients

### Meat Loaf
- 1 kg / 2 lb beef mince (or lamb mince, or 50/50 blend)
- 1 large onion, grated
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp ground cumin
- 1 tbsp ground coriander
- 1 tsp smoked paprika
- 1 tsp cayenne pepper (adjust to taste)
- 1 tsp salt
- ½ tsp black pepper
- 2 tbsp olive oil

### To Serve
- Warm pide or flatbread
- Shredded lettuce
- Sliced tomatoes
- Sliced onion
- Pickled chillies
- Garlic yogurt sauce or tahini sauce
- Lemon wedges

---

## Instructions

1. **Preheat oven** to 180°C / 350°F. Line a large loaf tin with baking paper.
2. **Combine** all meat loaf ingredients in a large bowl. Mix well with hands until evenly combined.
3. **Press** mixture firmly into the loaf tin, smoothing the top.
4. **Bake** for 1 hour until firm and cooked through. The top should be deeply browned.
5. **Rest** for 15 minutes before slicing. This helps it hold together.
6. **Slice thinly** using a sharp knife or electric carving knife, just like a kebab shop.
7. **Serve** in warm bread with salad and sauces.

---

## Tips

- **Grate the onion** rather than dicing — it breaks down during cooking and helps bind the loaf.
- **Don't skip the resting time** — it firms up and slices much more cleanly.
- **For extra char**: Broil the top for 3–5 minutes at the end of baking.
- **Leftovers**: Keeps 3–4 days in the fridge. Reheat slices in a hot pan for best texture.
- **Freezer friendly**: Wrap tightly and freeze for up to 3 months.

---

## Nutrition (per serving, serves 6)

| Nutrient | Amount |
|---|---|
| Calories | 380 cal |
| Protein | 35 g |
| Carbohydrates | 5 g |
| Fat | 24 g |
| Sodium | 520 mg |

name: Meatloaf (Extra Delicious)
content:
# Meatloaf Recipe (Extra Delicious!)

**By Nagi Maehashi** | Published: May 23, 2020 | Updated: May 9, 2025
**Rating**: 4.96/5 (from 486 votes) | **Servings**: 8
**Prep**: 15 min | **Cook**: 1 hr 15 min | **Total**: 1 hr 30 min

> *"This is a Meatloaf recipe for people who love their Meatloaf oozing with flavour, moist and tender yet not crumble apart when sliced, and a sticky caramelised meatloaf glaze."*

---

## Key Secrets to a Superior Meatloaf

- **Use fatty beef** (at least 15% fat) for juiciness.
- **Grate your onion** instead of dicing — this is a game-changer:
  - Breadcrumbs absorb onion juices, puff up during baking → **tender texture**.
  - Adds deep savory flavor without pre-frying.
- **Boost flavor with beef bouillon cubes** (not just salt) — delivers umami depth.
- **Use a loaf pan** for even cooking, moisture retention, and structural integrity.

> *"The secret to making a meatloaf taste really good is to use fatty beef, plenty of flavourings and to grate your onion instead of dicing it."*

---

## Ingredients

### Meatloaf Base:
- 1 cup **panko breadcrumbs**
- 1 large **onion**, grated (brown/yellow)
- 1 kg / 2 lb **ground beef** (not lean)
- 2 **eggs**
- 3 **garlic cloves**, minced
- 1 tsp **Worcestershire sauce**
- ¼ cup **tomato ketchup**
- ¼ cup **chopped parsley** (or 2 tsp dried parsley/basil)
- 1 tsp **dried thyme**
- 2 **beef bouillon cubes**, crumbled (or 2 tsp beef powder)
- 1 tsp **black pepper**

### Meatloaf Glaze:
- ½ cup **tomato ketchup**
- 2 tbsp **cider vinegar**
- 1 tbsp **brown sugar**, lightly packed

---

## Instructions

1. Preheat oven to **180°C / 350°F**. Oil or spray a **loaf tin** (~23 x 13 x 6 cm / 9 x 5 x 2.5").
2. **Make glaze**: Mix all glaze ingredients; set aside.
3. **Prepare base**: In a large bowl, combine panko and grated onion. Mix until breadcrumbs are wet.
4. Add all remaining meatloaf ingredients. **Mix well with hands**.
5. Form into a loaf shape, smoothing cracks (some cracks are good — glaze seeps in!).
6. Place in loaf pan. Brush with **half the glaze**.
7. **Bake 45 min** → remove, brush with most of remaining glaze. Drain excess fat if needed.
8. **Bake 30 more min** → total **1 hr 15 min**.
9. **Rest 10 min** before slicing. Slight edge crumble = perfect tenderness!

> *"You will probably get a bit of crumbling just on the edges of the first couple of slices, this is normal and indicative of the tenderness..."*

---

## Recipe Notes

- **Panko breadcrumbs**: Larger than regular → softer texture. Found in most supermarkets.
- **Bouillon substitute**: Chicken/veg bouillon or **1.5 tsp salt** (less flavorful).
- **Pan size**: Standard loaf pan works; shape may vary slightly.
- **Grating onion**: Critical technique — enhances tenderness and flavor distribution.

---

## FAQ & Pro Tips

| Question | Answer |
|--------|--------|
| **Why does meatloaf fall apart?** | Under-mixing or not pressing firmly. Use a loaf pan! Cracks are okay — glaze fills them. |
| **Must I use a loaf pan?** | **Yes, strongly recommended**. Freeform loaves overcook edges and dry out. |
| **Cover while baking?** | **No** — you want browning and caramelization. Tent with foil only if browning too fast. |
| **Cook time for 1kg/2lb?** | **1 hr 15 min at 180°C / 350°F**. |

---

## Serving Suggestions

- **Classic**: Creamy mashed potatoes + steamed greens + ketchup dollop.
- **Low-carb**: Cauliflower mash.
- **Leftovers**: Make **excellent sandwiches**!

---

## Nutrition per serving (179g)

- Calories: 404 | Protein: 24g | Fat: 26g | Carbs: 15g
- Sodium: 607mg | Cholesterol: 129mg

name: Cottage Pie
content:
# Cottage Pie

**By Nagi Maehashi** | Published: 21 May '20 | Updated: 9 May '25
🔗 [Source](https://www.recipetineats.com/cottage-pie/)

---

## Overview

> **"Cottage Pie – comfort food central! This English classic has a deeply savoury beef mince filling smothered in gravy topped with creamy mashed potato, baked until golden. One of the best cosy foods to make with ground beef!"**

- **Cottage Pie vs. Shepherd's Pie**:
  - **Cottage Pie** = made with **beef**
  - **Shepherd's Pie** = made with **lamb** *(because shepherds herd sheep!)*
- Universally loved, economical, freezer-friendly, and perfect for winter (or year-round!).

**Prep:** 30 min | **Cook:** 1 hr | **Total:** 1 hr 30 min | **Serves:** 5

---

## Ingredients

### Beef Filling
- 1½ tbsp olive oil
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 1 carrot, finely chopped
- 1 celery, finely chopped
- 750g / 1.5 lb beef mince (ground beef)
- ¼ cup (40g) plain flour
- ¼ cup (55g) tomato paste
- 2 cups (500ml) low-sodium beef stock
- ½ cup (125ml) red wine (or water)
- 1 beef bouillon cube (or 1 tsp stock powder)
- 2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
- 1 tsp dried thyme (or 3 sprigs fresh)
- 2 dried bay leaves
- ¾ tsp salt
- ½ tsp black pepper

### Mashed Potato Topping
- 1.2 kg / 2.5 lb potatoes, peeled & cubed
- ⅔ cup (165ml) warm milk
- 2 tbsp (30g) butter

### Optional Parmesan Crust
- 2 tbsp (30g) butter
- 2 tbsp (20g) grated parmesan

---

## Instructions

1. **Cook aromatics**: Heat oil → sauté onion & garlic (1 min) → add carrot & celery → cook 3 mins until soft.
2. **Brown beef**: Increase heat → add beef → cook until browned, breaking up lumps.
3. **Build sauce**: Stir in flour → add tomato paste, stock, wine, bouillon, Worcestershire, thyme, bay leaves, salt, pepper.
4. **Simmer**: Bring to simmer → cook **30 mins** on medium-high until reduced to **gravy consistency** (critical!).
5. **Cool filling**: Transfer to pie dish → cool (even overnight). *Prevents potato from sinking.*
6. **Make mash**: Boil potatoes 15 mins → drain → steam dry 30 sec → mash with butter, milk, salt until smooth.
7. **Assemble**: Spread mash over cooled filling → rough up surface with fork → sprinkle parmesan + drizzle butter/oil.
8. **Bake**: 180°C/350°F for **25–30 mins** until golden & bubbling.
9. **Rest**: Stand 5 mins before serving. Garnish with fresh thyme if desired.

---

## Key Tips for Success

✅ **Cool the filling** before topping with mash → prevents sinking.
✅ **Reduce filling properly** → final sauce thickness = post-bake thickness (no evaporation in oven).
✅ **Steam-dry potatoes** → avoids watery filling.
✅ **Use hot mash** → easier to spread.
✅ **Rough up mash surface** = more golden, crispy bits!

---

## Make Ahead & Freezer Guide

- **Refrigerate**: Assembled (unbaked) or cooked → up to **4 days**.
- **Freeze**:
  - Unbaked: Freeze assembled pie → **thaw before baking** (baking from frozen takes ~1 hr).
  - Baked leftovers: Freeze well.
- **Individual portions**: Use ramekins for easy freezing/reheating.

---

## Serving Suggestions

Pair with fresh sides to balance richness:
- Rocket salad + balsamic dressing
- Garden salad + French/Italian vinaigrette
- Glazed roasted carrots
- Sautéed garlic spinach

---

name: Irish Beef and Guinness Stew
content:
# Irish Beef and Guinness Stew

**Author:** Nagi Maehashi | **Published:** 15 Mar '20 | **Updated:** 9 May '25
**Rating:** 4.99/5 (501 votes) | **Servings:** 6 | **Total Time:** 3 hrs 10 mins

---

## Overview

> "There's no greater comfort food than a hearty stew. And Irish Beef and Guinness Stew might be the king of them all! Guinness gives the sauce an incredible rich, deep flavour, and the beef is fall-apart tender."

> "Arguably the most deeply flavourful sauce of all stews, with a rich dark brown flavour, this is the best of the best."

This is a reader-favourite recipe included in Nagi's debut cookbook **"Dinner"** by popular demand.

---

## Ingredients

| Ingredient | Amount | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Beef chuck (or boneless short rib) | 2.5 lb / 1.25 kg | Cut into **5cm / 2" chunks** — must be large pieces |
| Olive oil | 2 tbsp | For browning |
| Salt & black pepper | ¾ tsp each | |
| Garlic cloves | 3, minced | |
| Onions | 2, chopped | Brown, white, or yellow |
| Bacon (or speck/pancetta) | 6 oz / 180g, diced | Adds extra flavour; can be skipped |
| Flour (all-purpose) | 3 tbsp | Thickens sauce |
| **Guinness Beer** | 440ml / 14.9 oz | **Key ingredient** |
| Tomato paste | 4 tbsp | |
| Chicken stock/broth | 3 cups (750ml) | Preferred over beef broth |
| Carrots | 3, cut into 1.25cm / ½" pieces | |
| Celery stalks | 2 large, cut into 2cm / 1" pieces | |
| Bay leaves | 2 | |
| Thyme | 3 sprigs (or 1 tsp dried) | |

---

## Instructions (Stovetop)

1. **Cut beef** into 5cm/2" chunks. Pat dry, season with salt and pepper.
2. **Brown the beef** in batches in a heavy-based pot over high heat. Remove to plate.
   *"Brown them well, this is key to flavour. It's not just the browned beef itself, also the brown bits left on the bottom of the pot (fond) adds extra flavour to the sauce."*
3. **Sauté flavour base:** Lower heat to medium. Cook garlic and onion 3 min until softening. Add bacon, cook until browned. Stir through carrot and celery.
4. **Cook off flour and tomato paste:** Stir flour for 1 minute.
5. **Add liquids:** Guinness, chicken broth, tomato paste, bay leaves, thyme. Mix well to dissolve flour.
6. **Return beef** to pot (with juices). Liquid should just cover the meat.
7. **Simmer covered** for 2 hours (beef should be pretty tender).
8. **Simmer uncovered** for a further 30–45 minutes until beef falls apart at a touch and sauce has reduced/thickened.
9. Skim fat, adjust seasoning, remove bay leaves and thyme.
10. **Serve** with creamy mashed potatoes!

---

## Alternative Cooking Methods

- **Oven:** Cover and bake 2½ hours at 160°C/320°F, then uncover 30–45 min to reduce.
- **Slow Cooker:** Reduce chicken broth by 1 cup. After adding liquids, bring to simmer, scrape pot bottom well. Transfer everything. Cook on LOW for 8 hours. If sauce needs thickening, reduce separately on stovetop.
- **Pressure Cooker:** Follow slow cooker instructions. Cook on HIGH for **40 minutes**.

---

## Important Recipe Notes

> **1. Guinness Beer:** "You CANNOT taste it in the finished dish, it just melds into an amazing sauce." No non-alcoholic substitute exists. If alcohol cannot be consumed: substitute Guinness with **2 cups water + 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce + 2 crumbled beef bouillon cubes**.

> **3. Flour:** Nagi prefers adding flour to thicken the sauce rather than dusting beef before browning, because "the flour burns then this permeates throughout the whole stew."

> **4. Beef vs Chicken Broth:** Chicken broth is preferred because its milder flavour lets the Guinness flavour come through more.

---

## Key Insights & Tips

- **Use big chunky hunks of beef** — not tiny cubes. Small pieces cook too quickly and fall apart before deep flavours develop.
- **Browning is critical** — both the beef and the fond (browned bits on the pot bottom) build the sauce's deep flavour.
- **All alcohol cooks out** — the finished dish does not taste like beer; it transforms into a deep savoury sauce.
- **Make-ahead friendly:** Can be made a day ahead; scoop off fat after refrigerating, then reheat and serve the next day.

---

## Serving Suggestions

- **Mashed potatoes** (classic pairing)
- **Cauliflower mash** (low-carb option)
- **Irish Soda Bread** (no-yeast) to mop the bowl clean

---

## Nutrition (per serving, excluding mashed potato)

| Protein | 72.2 g |
| Carbohydrates | 15.3 g |
| Fat | 29.1 g |
| Saturated Fat | 9.2 g |
| Cholesterol | 200 mg |
| Sodium | 1499 mg |

name: Baked Ziti
content:
# Baked Ziti

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/baked-ziti/) | **Author:** Nagi Maehashi
**Rating:** 4.96 from 172 votes | **Servings:** 6
**Prep:** 10 min | **Cook:** 1 hr | **Total:** 1 hr 10 min

---

## Overview

> "This is not just another pasta bake. This is the mother of all pasta bakes!"

Baked Ziti is an American pasta bake with tomato-based meat sauce similar to Bolognese. "Ziti" is smooth-surfaced pasta resembling penne (which has ridges). Italian-American dish with centuries-old Italian origins.

---

## Ingredients

### Main Components
- **Pasta**: 300g/10oz ziti or penne (ziti is smooth; penne has ridges)
- **Meat**: 500g/1 lb ground beef, pork, or 50/50 combo (pork adds richness)
- **Tomato Passata**: 700g/24oz (called "tomato puree" in America)
- **Ricotta**: 1 cup (optional but recommended for authenticity)
- **Cheeses**: 1 cup grated mozzarella + 1 cup freshly grated parmesan

### Seasonings
- 2 tsp fennel seeds (optional)
- 1 tbsp paprika (plain or sweet)
- 1/4 tsp cayenne pepper OR 3/4 tsp chilli flakes
- 2 tsp onion powder
- 1 tsp EACH salt and black pepper
- 1 tsp EACH dried basil, oregano, sugar

---

## Instructions

1. **Cook ziti**: Boil for recommended time MINUS 2 minutes; drain and return to pot
2. **Preheat oven**: 180C/350F
3. **Make sauce**:
   - Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil (2 minutes)
   - Add meat, cook until done
   - Add seasonings, cook 2 minutes
   - Add passata, water, basil, oregano, sugar; simmer 10 minutes
4. **Assemble**:
   - Toss ~2 cups sauce with ziti
   - Spread in baking dish (23x33cm/9×13")
   - Dollop with ricotta
   - Pour remaining sauce over top
   - Top with mozzarella and parmesan
5. **Bake**:
   - Cover loosely with foil → 20 minutes
   - Remove foil → 10-15 minutes until cheese is golden

---

## Quick Tips & Substitutions

### Make Ahead & Storage
- Cook pasta and sauce separately, cool, then assemble
- Refrigerate or freeze before baking
- If frozen, defrost 24 hours in fridge before baking
- Bake from cold: ~30-35 minutes (25 covered + 10 uncovered)

### Substitutions
- **Pasta**: Penne, rigatoni, spirals, macaroni, or small shells
- **Tomato passata**:
  - 800g can crushed tomato + 2 tbsp tomato paste
  - Blended canned tomatoes
  - Store-bought marinara or American "tomato sauce"
- **Ricotta**: Optional; use creamy variety (add milk/cream if crumbly)

---

## Serving Suggestions

- **Garlic Bread**: Use basic French stick with plenty of butter
- **Side Salads**:
  - Arugula/Rocket Salad with Balsamic Dressing and Shaved Parmesan
  - Cherry Tomato Salad with Basil
  - Cucumber Salad with Herb Garlic Vinaigrette
  - Caprese Salad
  - Green Bean Salad with Cherry Tomatoes and Feta

name: Chinese Beef with Honey and Black Pepper Sauce
content:
# Beef Stir Fry with Honey Pepper Sauce

**Author:** Nagi Maehashi | **Published:** 4 Nov '19 | **Updated:** 21 Jun '25 | **Rating:** 4.97/5 (176 votes) | **Servings:** 4 | **Total Time:** 15 minutes

---

## Overview

> "A Beef Stir Fry to die for! Tender strips of beef with a sticky **honey black pepper stir fry sauce**, this is one of my favourite dishes at my local Chinese restaurant. This may well be the **fastest stir fry recipe** you make all year – 1 minute to cook the beef, 1 minute to make the sauce. And you're DONE!"

A copycat of restaurant-style Chinese Beef with Honey and Black Pepper Sauce. The sauce is described as **"Sweet, salty and with a subtle peppery heat"** — intensely flavored and syrupy (not cornstarch-thickened like most stir fry sauces).

**Prep:** 10 min | **Cook:** 5 min | **Total:** 15 min

---

## Ingredients

### Sauce
| Ingredient | Amount | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Soy sauce | 2½ tbsp | All-purpose or light soy; **not** dark soy |
| Honey | 3 tbsp | Primary sweetener |
| Oyster sauce | 1½ tbsp | Sub: Hoisin |
| Chinese cooking wine (Shaoxing) | 1½ tbsp | Sub: Mirin, dry sherry, cooking sake; Non-alc: chicken broth |
| Water | 2 tbsp | Omit if using broth sub |
| Black pepper (coarsely crushed) | 1 tsp | Or ½ tsp ground black pepper |

### Stir Fry
| Ingredient | Amount | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Peanut oil (or veg/canola) | 2 tbsp | |
| Garlic clove | 1, finely minced | |
| Onion | ½, peeled & sliced | |
| Beef (tenderloin, flank, sirloin, etc.) | 500g / 1 lb, thinly sliced | See beef notes below |

---

## Instructions

1. **Mix** all Sauce ingredients in a bowl.
2. **Heat** oil in a wok or large heavy skillet over **high heat** until smoking.
3. **Add** onion and garlic; cook **1 minute** until onion is translucent (keep garlic moving to avoid burning).
4. **Add** beef; stir fry **1 minute** until just cooked. **Remove** beef to a bowl.
5. **Reduce** heat to medium-high. **Pour in Sauce** — it will simmer quickly. Cook **~1 minute** until syrupy (larger bubbles, caramel color).
6. **Return** beef and any pooled juices to wok. Toss until just warmed through — **1 minute max**. Do not overcook.
7. **Serve immediately** with rice (or cauliflower rice for low-carb).

---

## Key Tips & Notes

### Beef Selection
> "Rule of thumb for stir fries: if you'd serve it as steak, it's ideal for stir fries!"

- **Best cuts:** Tenderloin, flank, New York strip/Porterhouse, sirloin, boneless ribeye/scotch fillet
- **Budget cuts** (e.g., chuck): Must be tenderized first using the Chinese "velveting" method
- **Slice against the grain** — cut perpendicular to the fiber direction for maximum tenderness
- **Other proteins:** Works well with pork, chicken, and turkey

### Sauce Characteristics
- This sauce is **intensely flavored and syrupy** — not cornstarch-thickened
- You don't need a large volume; the flavor is concentrated
- If sauce is too watery, cook longer to reduce to a glaze

### Serving Suggestions
- Serve with **rice** or **Cauliflower Rice** (77% fewer calories, 87% fewer carbs)
- Pair with a fresh side: **Asian Slaw**, **Chinese Lettuce with Creamy Sesame Dressing**, or **Asian Side Salad**
- Add veggies if desired: julienned carrots, capsicum, broccoli, snap peas
- For restaurant-style sizzle: use a preheated cast iron dish on a wooden base

---

name: Slow Cooker Red Wine Beef Cheeks
content:
# Slow Cooker Red Wine Beef Cheeks

**Source:** [RecipeTin Eats](https://www.recipetineats.com/slow-cooker-red-wine-beef-cheeks/) | **Author:** Nagi Maehashi

---

## Overview

Fall-apart tender beef cheeks braised in a rich red wine sauce with aromatic vegetables. This is French bistro comfort food at its finest — deeply flavored, luxurious, and surprisingly easy when made in a slow cooker.

**Prep:** 20 min | **Cook:** 8 hrs (slow cooker) | **Total:** 8 hrs 20 min | **Serves:** 6

---

## Ingredients

### Beef & Marinade
- 1.5 kg / 3 lb beef cheeks, trimmed of excess fat
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- Salt and black pepper
- 2 tbsp plain flour

### Braising Liquid
- 2 cups (500ml) red wine (Burgundy, Shiraz, or Cabernet)
- 2 cups (500ml) beef stock
- 4 tbsp tomato paste
- 2 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
- 2 bay leaves
- 4 sprigs fresh thyme
- 2 sprigs fresh rosemary

### Aromatics
- 2 onions, roughly chopped
- 3 carrots, cut into chunks
- 3 celery stalks, cut into chunks
- 4 garlic cloves, smashed
- 200g / 7 oz bacon lardons (optional)

### To Finish
- 2 tbsp butter
- 2 tbsp flour (for beurre manié, optional)
- Fresh parsley, chopped

---

## Instructions

1. **Pat beef cheeks dry** and season generously with salt and pepper. Dust lightly with flour.
2. **Sear the beef:** Heat olive oil in a large skillet over high heat. Sear beef cheeks on all sides until deeply browned (about 3 minutes per side). Transfer to slow cooker.
3. **Sauté aromatics:** In the same skillet, add bacon (if using), onions, carrots, celery, and garlic. Cook for 5 minutes until softened and lightly browned.
4. **Deglaze:** Pour in red wine, scraping up all the browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Let it simmer for 2 minutes.
5. **Combine:** Add stock, tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, bay leaves, thyme, and rosemary. Stir to combine. Pour over beef cheeks in the slow cooker.
6. **Slow cook:** Cover and cook on LOW for 8 hours (or HIGH for 4–5 hours) until beef cheeks are fall-apart tender.
7. **Remove beef** to a plate and tent with foil.
8. **Reduce sauce:** Transfer the braising liquid and vegetables to a saucepan. Simmer over medium-high heat for 15–20 minutes until reduced to a rich, coating consistency. For extra thickness, mash some of the vegetables into the sauce or whisk in a beurre manié (equal parts butter and flour).
9. **Season** to taste with salt and pepper.
10. **Serve** beef cheeks over creamy mashed potatoes or polenta, spooning the rich red wine sauce generously over the top. Garnish with fresh parsley.

---

## Tips

- **Beef cheeks are the star** — they become incredibly tender with slow cooking and have a rich, gelatinous texture.
- **Don't skip the searing step** — it builds the deep, caramelized flavor base.
- **Wine choice matters** — use a wine you'd happily drink. A medium-bodied red like Shiraz or Côtes du Rhône works beautifully.
- **Make ahead:** This dish tastes even better the next day. Refrigerate and reheat gently.
- **Freezer friendly:** Freeze with the sauce for up to 3 months.

---

## Serving Suggestions

- Creamy mashed potatoes (classic)
- Soft polenta
- Buttered egg noodles
- Crusty bread to mop up the sauce
- Steamed green beans or roasted broccolini

---

## Nutrition (per serving, serves 6)

| Calories | 520 cal |
| Protein | 48 g |
| Carbohydrates | 12 g |
| Fat | 28 g |
| Saturated Fat | 11 g |
| Cholesterol | 165 mg |
| Sodium | 680 mg |
| Fiber | 2 g |
| Sugar | 5 g |

name: Thai Beef Salad
content:
# Thai Beef Salad

**By Nagi Maehashi** | Published: 12 Aug '19 | Updated: 9 May '25
🔗 [Source](https://www.recipetineats.com/thai-beef-salad-2/)

---

## Overview

A **restaurant-quality Thai Beef Salad** inspired by **Spice I Am**, one of the best Thai restaurants outside Thailand. This recipe stands out for its **authentic dressing made with coriander (cilantro) stems**, delivering bold, fresh, zingy flavors typical of Asian salads—without mayo, cream, or cheese.

> "Not just another Thai Beef Salad… This is made with one little change to create a dressing from an award-winning Thai restaurant."

Perfect for using high-quality steak efficiently—only **200–250g (7–8 oz)** needed for two servings.

**Prep:** 20 min | **Cook:** 5 min | **Total:** 25 min | **Serves:** 2

---

## Ingredients

### Dressing
- ½ tsp bird's eye or Thai chili (deseeded, finely minced)
- ¼ tsp finely minced garlic (~½ small clove)
- 1 tbsp finely chopped **coriander/cilantro stems**
- 2¼ tsp white sugar
- 2 tbsp fish sauce
- 3 tbsp lime juice
- 1 tbsp grapeseed, canola, or vegetable oil
- Pinch of kosher/cooking salt

### Salad
- 200–250g (7–8 oz) good-quality beef steak *(e.g., sirloin, ribeye, flat iron, rump)*
- 1 tbsp oil (for searing)
- Salt & pepper
- 2 heaped cups mixed lettuce
- 10 cherry tomatoes, halved
- ¼ small red onion, very finely sliced
- ½ cucumber (optional, deseeded), sliced (~⅓ cup)
- ¼ cup cilantro leaves (lightly packed)
- ¼ cup mint leaves (lightly packed)

### Garnish
- ¼ cup roasted unsalted peanuts, finely chopped
- Extra cilantro & mint leaves

---

## Instructions

### 1. Make the Dressing
- **Best method**: Grind chili, garlic, coriander stems, and salt into a **paste using a mortar and pestle**.
- Add remaining dressing ingredients; adjust sugar, lime, and fish sauce to taste.
- **No mortar?** Finely mince ingredients, smear into paste with knife, then shake in a jar.

### 2. Cook the Beef
- Heat skillet until **screaming hot and smoking**.
- Oil both sides of steak; season with salt and pepper.
- **Cook times (for 2cm / 4/5" thick steak)**:
  - **Medium rare**: 2 min per side → internal temp **52°C / 125°F**
  - **Medium**: 2.5 min per side → **57°C / 135°F**
- Rest beef **10 minutes tented with foil** before slicing.

> 💡 **Pro tip**: Always slice **against the grain** for maximum tenderness. Look for muscle fibers—cut perpendicular to their direction.

### 3. Assemble Salad
- Toss lettuce with **1 tbsp dressing**.
- Slice beef thinly; combine with other salad ingredients and **most of remaining dressing**.
- Plate: lettuce base → topped with beef mixture.
- Garnish with peanuts, herbs, and drizzle remaining dressing.
- **Serve immediately**.

---

## Recipe Notes

1. **Chili level**:
   - ½ tsp = mildly spicy
   - 1 tsp = decently spicy
   - ¼ tsp = mild

2. **Steak choice**: Any grill-friendly cut works—sirloin, ribeye, porterhouse, flank, flat iron (author's favorite), or rump.

3. **Resting is critical**: Beef continues cooking while resting. You can always cook more—but never uncook!

4. **Dressing yield**: Makes extra! Store leftover in fridge for future use (great on grilled veggies or noodles).

---

## Nutrition (per serving, serves 2)

- Calories: 384 kcal
- Protein: 38.1g
- Carbs: 13.5g
- Fat: 19.9g
- Sodium: 1553mg
- Iron: 24.8mg (138% DV)

name: Mexican Shredded Beef (Tacos)
content:
# Mexican Shredded Beef (and Tacos)

**Author:** Nagi Maehashi (RecipeTin Eats)
**Published:** July 12, 2019 | **Updated:** May 9, 2025
**Rating:** 4.96/5 from 283 votes
**Servings:** 20 tacos (serves 8–10 people)
**Total Time:** 3 hours 20 minutes (20 min prep + 3 hours cook)

> *"This Mexican Shredded Beef has incredible depth of flavour! The sauce is really rich and thick, and there is PLENTY of it."*

---

## Key Highlights

- **Secret ingredient:** Orange juice – common in Mexican cuisine. Doesn't taste orangey after slow cooking; adds sweetness and depth.
- **Multi-cooker friendly:** Stovetop, oven, slow cooker, or pressure cooker/Instant Pot
- **Freezer friendly:** Keep sauce and beef separate for best texture when defrosting
- **Versatile:** Tacos, burritos, enchiladas, quesadillas, nachos, sliders, and more
- **Featured in Nagi's debut cookbook *"Dinner"*** by popular demand

---

## Ingredients

### Spice Mix
| Ingredient | Amount |
|---|---|
| Chipotle powder | 1 1/2 tbsp (adjustable) |
| Paprika | 1 tbsp |
| Dried oregano | 1 tbsp |
| All Spice powder | 1 tsp |
| Coriander powder | 1 tsp |
| Onion powder or garlic powder | 2 tsp (or 1 tsp each) |
| Salt and pepper | 1 tsp each |

### Beef & Braising Liquid
| Ingredient | Amount |
|---|---|
| Olive oil | 1–2 tbsp |
| Beef chuck or brisket | 3 lb / 1.5 kg, cut into 4 pieces |
| Garlic cloves | 5, minced |
| Onion | 1, diced |
| Orange juice | 3/4 cup (185 ml) |
| Lime juice | 2 tbsp |
| Crushed tomatoes | 14 oz / 400g can |
| Beef or chicken broth | 2 cups (500 ml) |
| Water | 1/2 cup (125 ml) |
| Salt and pepper | To taste |

---

## Instructions

1. **Season beef:** Combine spice mix; sprinkle 4 tsp over beef pieces and pat to adhere.
2. **Sear aggressively:** Heat oil in heavy pot over high heat. Brown beef well on all sides (in batches if needed). Remove to plate.
3. **Sauté aromatics:** Reduce heat to medium. Add garlic and onion; cook 3 minutes until soft.
4. **Deglaze:** Add orange juice and lime juice, scraping browned bits from pot bottom.
5. **Add liquids:** Add crushed tomatoes, broth, water, and remaining spice mix. Stir to combine.
6. **Braise:** Return beef to pot. Cover, bring to simmer, reduce to gentle bubble.
7. **Cook:** 2 hours covered, then remove lid and simmer 30 more minutes until shreddable tender.
8. **Shred beef:** Remove beef from sauce; shred with 2 forks.
9. **Reduce sauce:** Simmer sauce uncovered 10–15 minutes to thicken. Adjust salt. *Optional:* Puree with stick blender for smooth sauce.
10. **Combine:** Toss shredded beef back into sauce (reserve some sauce for drizzling if desired).

---

## Cooking Method Variations

| Method | Time & Notes |
|--------|-------------|
| **Stovetop** | 2.5–3 hours total as written |
| **Oven** | 3–3.5 hours at 325°F/160°C |
| **Slow Cooker** | 8 hours on LOW. Reduce sauce on stovetop after cooking |
| **Pressure Cooker / Instant Pot** | 50 minutes on HIGH. Same sauce-reduction step needed |

---

## Chipotle Powder Heat Guide

| Level | Chipotle Powder |
|-------|----------------|
| **Mild** | 2 tsp – 1 tbsp |
| **Medium** | 1 1/2 tbsp |
| **Spicy** | 2 tbsp |

**Substitute if unavailable:** 1/2 tbsp each smoked paprika, cumin, and cayenne pepper.

---

## Serving Suggestions

### Classic Taco Spread
- Warmed/charred tortillas
- Pico de Gallo
- Sour cream
- Avocado slices
- Lime wedges
- Fresh coriander/cilantro

### Other Uses
- **Burritos** (freezer friendly)
- **Beef Enchiladas**
- **Quesadillas**
- **Nachos**
- **Mexican Red Rice bowls**
- **Sliders** in bread rolls
- **Empanadas** with puff pastry
- **Mexican Cottage Pie** (topped with mashed potato)
- **Mexican Beef Lasagna** (layered with tortillas and cheese)

---

## Storage & Reheating

- **Refrigerate/Freeze:** Keep beef and **sauce separate** to prevent sogginess.
- **Reheat:** Microwave or oven (foil-covered) for beef; heat sauce separately, then toss together.
- **Yield:** Enough for 20–25 tacos or 12–15 burritos.

---

| Nutrient | Amount | % Daily Value |
|----------|--------|--------------|
| Calories | 372 cal | 19% |
| Protein | 38g | 76% |
| Fat | 18g | 28% |
| Carbs | 15g | 5% |
| Sodium | 890mg | 39% |

content:
# Asian Beef Bowls (Super Quick!)

**Author:** Nagi Maehashi | **Published:** 5 Jun '19 | **Updated:** 11 May '25 | **Rating:** 4.97/5 (249 votes) | **Servings:** 4 | **Total Time:** 12 minutes (5 min prep + 7 min cook)

---

## Overview

A fast, flavorful stir-fry made with **beef mince (ground beef)** and 100% authentic Asian condiments. Not a traditional recipe, but the sauce closely mirrors classic Chinese stir-fry flavors. Designed for busy weeknights when you need dinner fast.

> *"Say goodbye to boring beef mince recipes….. these Asian Beef Bowls are super tasty, super easy and SUPER fast!"*

**Key selling points:**
- No meat slicing required — uses ground beef
- Made entirely with authentic Asian ingredients (no Western substitutions in the sauce)
- Juicy and flavorful despite not being a "saucy" stir-fry
- Beef mince can be swapped with chicken, pork, or turkey

**Prep:** 5 min | **Cook:** 7 min | **Total:** 12 min

---

## Ingredients

### Sauce
| Ingredient | Amount | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Hoisin Sauce | 1 tbsp | Or sub with Oyster Sauce |
| Oyster Sauce | 2 tbsp | Or sub with 1½ tbsp Hoisin |
| Chinese cooking wine | 1 tbsp | Or Mirin, dry sherry, sake, rice wine |
| Dark soy sauce | 2½ tbsp | See Note 2 |
| White sugar | 2 tsp | |
| Sesame oil | ½ tsp | |
| White pepper | ¼ tsp | Or sub with black pepper |

### Stir Fry
| Ingredient | Amount |
|---|---|
| Oil | 1 tbsp |
| Garlic, minced | 2 cloves |
| Onion, finely chopped | 1 small |
| Beef mince (ground beef) | 500g / 1 lb (lean is good) |
| Shallots / scallions, sliced | ½ cup + more for garnish |

### Garnishes
- Sesame seeds

---

## Instructions

1. **Mix Sauce** together in a bowl.
2. **Heat oil** in a large skillet over **high heat**. Add garlic and onion, cook for **1 minute** or until onion starts browning.
3. **Add beef** and cook, breaking it up as you go.
4. **Once beef has changed from red to brown**, add Sauce. Cook for **2 more minutes**, or until Sauce has evaporated and beef is cooked. *(For more cooked beef, add a splash of water and keep cooking.)*
5. **Stir through shallots**, then serve over rice, garnished with more shallots and sesame seeds.

---

## Important Notes

> **Note 1 — Chinese cooking wine:** Sub with Mirin, dry sherry, or cooking sake. If you can't consume alcohol, add **¼ cup chicken stock/broth** — sauce will take ~3 minutes to evaporate, but the end result will be similar.

> **Note 2 — Soy Sauce:** This recipe calls for **DARK soy sauce** — it has more flavour than light/all-purpose soy sauce and gives the food a deeper brown colour. Can sub with all-purpose soy, but beef colour won't be as dark brown.

> **Note 3 — Storage:** Leftovers keep in the fridge for **4 days**, or freeze for up to **3 months**.

---

## Serving Suggestions

- **Over rice** (standard) or **Cauliflower Rice** (77% fewer calories, 87% fewer carbs)
- **Side salads:** Smashed Cucumber Salad, Asian Slaw, Asian Side Salad, Asian Sesame Dressing
- **Popular variation:** Asian Beef Ramen Noodles — the noodle version of this recipe

---

## Reader Adaptations & Tips

- **Extra sauce:** Many double the sauce quantity for more sauciness
- **Added vegetables:** Spinach, red capsicum, zucchini, bok choy, mushrooms, peppers, snow peas, grated carrot, cabbage, bean sprouts
- **Protein swaps:** Pork mince, chicken mince, turkey mince all work well
- **Extra aromatics:** Fresh ginger, red chili peppers/paste
- **Serving additions:** Soft fried egg on top, sushi rice seasoning

---

## Nutrition (per serving, excluding rice)

| Nutrient | Amount | % Daily Value |
|---|---|---|
| Calories | 302 cal | 15% |
| Carbohydrates | 9 g | 3% |
| Protein | 26 g | 52% |
| Fat | 16 g | 25% |
| Saturated Fat | 5 g | 31% |
| Cholesterol | 81 mg | 27% |
| Sodium | 1024 mg | 45% |
| Potassium | 499 mg | 14% |
| Sugar | 4 g | 4% |
| Iron | 3.3 mg | 18% |

name: Skirt Steak with Chimichurri Sauce
content:
# Skirt Steak with Chimichurri Sauce

**Author:** Nagi Maehashi (RecipeTin Eats)
**Cuisine:** Argentinian
**Rating:** 4.92 from 37 votes
**Servings:** 4
**Total Time:** 15 minutes (Prep: 5 min, Cook: 10 min)

---

## Overview

> "Chimichurri Sauce and Steak are a match made in heaven. Like tomato and basil. Cheese and wine. They are just 'meant to be'."

> "I think of Chimichurri as South America's answer to pesto. Except…..dare I say it… _better_."

> "By itself, Chimichurri Sauce is kind of bland. However, when you eat it with steak, that is when the magic happens."

> "**Flat Iron is my personal favourite** – it's softly marbled with fat so it's tender and has a great beefy flavour. I prefer it over more expensive cuts like tenderloin – better texture and flavour!"

**Prep:** 5 min | **Cook:** 10 min | **Total:** 15 min | **Serves:** 4

---

## Ingredients

### Chimichurri Sauce
- 1 cup parsley leaves, tightly packed
- 1 tbsp oregano leaves, tightly packed (still lovely without)
- 4 garlic cloves, minced
- 0-2 tsp red pepper flakes (optional – adjust to taste)
- 1/4 cup (65 ml) red wine vinegar
- 1/2 tsp salt
- Black pepper
- 1/2 cup (125 ml) extra virgin olive oil

### Steak
- 700g / 1.4lb flat iron, flank, skirt steak or other steak of choice, at room temperature
- Salt and pepper

---

## Instructions

### Chimichurri Sauce
1. Place all ingredients except oil in a food processor. Whizz until parsley is finely chopped, but not pureed. Alternatively chop parsley by hand.
2. Transfer into a small bowl. Add oil, gently stir. Set aside for 1 hour before use, overnight is even better!
3. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator and use within 3 days. Makes 3/4 cup.

### Steak
1. Take steak out of the fridge 30 minutes prior to cooking.
2. Heat BBQ or heavy based skillet with 1 tbsp vegetable or canola oil over high heat until smoking.
3. Sprinkle each side of steak generously with salt and pepper.
4. Add steak and cook to your liking.
5. **Cook times:** for steak 2cm / 4/5" thick:
   - **Medium rare**: 2 min on each side (internal temp 52C/125F, will rise to 54C/130F after resting)
   - **Medium**: 2 1/2 min each side (internal temp 57C/135F, will rise to 60C/140F)
6. Transfer to a plate, cover loosely with foil and rest for 5-10 minutes.
7. Cut thin slices against the grain and serve with Chimichurri Sauce on the side.

---

## Recipe Notes

1. **Vinegar:** Can be substituted with sherry vinegar or other mild vinegar. Don't use balsamic or normal white vinegar – too harsh.
2. **Salt:** There is not that much salt in the Chimichurri Sauce, the assumption is that it's served with a well seasoned steak.
3. **Best steak for Chimichurri Sauce:** In South America, cuts such as flank, flat iron and skirt are popular because they have really great beefy flavour that pairs well with this fresh, zingy sauce. However, Chimichurri will pair beautifully with ANY steak!
4. **Slicing Steak:** Chimichurri Steak is traditionally served sliced. No matter what steak you use — but this is especially important for skirt, flank, baguette, flat iron and similar South American cuts, ensure you slice thinly against the grain. To do this, place the cooked steak in front of you and you'll see the fibres running in one direction. Cut directly through these fibres. This ensures every bite is beautifully tender.
5. **Storage:** Because Chimichurri sauce has raw garlic in it, it should be consumed within 3 days, for food safety reasons. Can be frozen too – thaw then use!

---

## Additional Tips

- Chimichurri is also terrific with chicken, turkey, fish, prawns/shrimp, and vegetables
- For best results, let sauce rest overnight before using
- When slicing meat, cut "across the grain" to ensure tenderness – especially important for skirt, flank, and flat iron steaks

---
